description: Daily product metric time-series extract for forecasting lessons.

tiny.description: Small deterministic daily series for hand checks and behavioral tests.

sample.description: Reserved for later lessons with longer history and rolling backtests.

metric_observations.grain: one metric, segment, and business date
known_defects: last observed date is intentionally incomplete
one historical value receives a revision after forecast origin
generation_rules: observed_date is the Europe/Moscow business date
period_start_at is the UTC instant of local midnight
published_at is a timezone-aware publication timestamp

columns.period_start_at: Timezone-aware timestamp at the start of the business period.
columns.published_at: Timezone-aware timestamp when the value became available.
columns.denominator: Metric denominator when the metric is a rate or share.
columns.is_complete_period: Whether the business period is closed for analysis.
columns.source_status: Source quality status: ok, partial, late, backfilled, or quality_hold.

backtest_observations.grain: one metric, segment, and complete historical business date for rolling-origin backtests
known_defects: the profile is still intentionally short for final 28-day model selection claims
generation_rules: values follow the same deterministic active_subscriptions pattern as the forecast tiny profile
all rows are complete because this table is used only for historical backtest origins
the table extends history without changing the final forecast scenario or current cutoff contract

columns.observed_date: Historical business date available for backtest training or actuals.
columns.is_complete_period: Whether the historical business period is complete.
columns.include_in_backtest: Whether the row is eligible for historical backtest windows.

subscription_events.grain: one subscription ledger delta event for a segment and business date
known_defects: last observed date is intentionally partial
generation_rules: occurred_at is stored as a UTC timestamp but belongs to the Europe/Moscow business date
delta_active reconciles opening balances to published active_subscriptions
events after complete_through are present but must be excluded from training by policy

columns.occurred_at: Timezone-aware event timestamp stored in UTC.
columns.available_at: Timezone-aware timestamp when the source event was available to analysts.
columns.event_type: Event type; tiny profile uses active_subscription_delta.
columns.delta_active: Signed change in active subscriptions for the segment.
columns.source_system: Source system that produced the event.
columns.ingestion_status: Source quality status: ok or partial in the tiny profile.

generation_rules: calendar covers the observed history and the 28-day forecast horizon
campaign and release flags are known before the forecast origin

columns.is_holiday: Holiday or internal calendar event flag.
columns.campaign_active: Whether a known marketing campaign is active.
columns.release_active: Whether a known release is active.
columns.payday_week: Whether the date belongs to a payday-like business week.
columns.support_capacity: Known support capacity for the date.
columns.known_before_date: Date when the calendar attribute was known to analysts.

generation_rules: release events are known before forecast origin

columns.known_before_date: Date when the release calendar became known.

generation_rules: campaign events are known before forecast origin

columns.known_before_date: Date when the campaign calendar became known.

data_revisions.grain: one revision of one metric, segment, and business date
known_defects: one revision arrives after forecast origin
generation_rules: revision 2 changes a historical value after the original publication

columns.observed_date: Business date of the revised value.

generation_rules: scenario fixes frequency, target segments, forecast origin, and horizon

columns.complete_through: Last date that must be complete.
columns.forecast_origin: Moment when the forecast is made.

resampling_spec.grain: one resampling setup for the forecast target
generation_rules: spec fixes timezone normalization, daily frequency, weekly label/closed policy, opening balances, and reconciliation tolerance

columns.source_time_column: Timestamp column to bucket into business dates.
columns.target_metric: Published metric that the resampled series must reconcile to.
columns.timezone: Business timezone used for timestamp bucketing.
columns.reconciliation_tolerance: Maximum allowed difference between resampled and published values.

window_feature_spec.grain: one leakage-safe window feature setup for the forecast target
generation_rules: all declared rolling and expanding features use strictly past observations
warmup rows and partial source rows are emitted for review but excluded from training

columns.time_column: Business-date column in the source series.
columns.value_column: Metric value used by lag, rolling, and expanding features.
columns.delta_column: Daily delta column used by delta lag features.
columns.complete_flag_column: Boolean source flag that controls training eligibility.
columns.feature_date_policy: Policy requiring features to use only observations before the feature date.
columns.rules: List of lag, rolling_mean, and expanding_mean feature rules.
columns.warmup_policy: Policy for rows without enough history.

seasonality_profile_spec.grain: one trend, seasonality, and calendar-effect profiling setup for the forecast target
generation_rules: seasonality profiles use complete historical rows only
calendar effects are allowed only when their calendar attributes are known before the forecast origin
monthly seasonality is marked as insufficient on the tiny profile because only one calendar month is observed

columns.source_table: Daily source table used for historical values.
columns.calendar_table: Calendar table joined by business date.
columns.target_metric: Metric whose trend and calendar profiles are described.
columns.time_column: Business-date column in the source series.
columns.value_column: Metric value used by trend and seasonal summaries.
columns.delta_column: Daily delta column summarized by seasonal profile.
columns.complete_flag_column: Boolean source flag that controls historical eligibility.
columns.calendar_date_column: Business-date column in the calendar table.
columns.seasonal_period_days: Declared weekly seasonal period in days.
columns.trend_policy: Policy for calculating the trend summary.
columns.seasonality_policy: Policy for grouping complete history into seasonal profiles.
columns.calendar_effect_columns: Calendar boolean columns that can become effect inventory rows.

temporal_leakage_spec.grain: one temporal leakage audit setup for a forecast origin
generation_rules: split plan is time-ordered and ends training at complete_through
partial rows after complete_through are embargoed rather than used for training
candidate features include selected safe features and rejected examples of target, future, centered-window, full-sample, random-split, and backfilled leakage

columns.target_metric: Metric whose forecast setup is audited.
columns.target_segments: Segments whose rows and features are audited.
columns.split_plan: Time-ordered cutoff, training window, embargo dates, and horizon.
columns.revision_policy: Policy for revisions published after forecast origin.
columns.known_future_feature_policy: Policy for known future calendar features.
columns.forbidden_availability_types: Availability classes that cannot be selected for modeling.
columns.candidate_features: Feature catalog with source, availability class, selection flag, and required evidence.
columns.quality_gates: Checks that must pass before forecasting lessons can use the setup.

baseline_forecast_spec.grain: one baseline forecast setup for a forecast origin
generation_rules: baseline models are declared before forecast evaluation
seasonal period is fixed at seven days from the calendar contract
simple baselines ignore known future campaign uplift and report that limitation

columns.forecast_id: Forecast scenario that the baselines belong to.
columns.source_table: Regular daily source table used by the baseline forecaster.
columns.cutoff_contract_id: Temporal leakage/cutoff contract that defines origin, training window, embargo, and horizon.
columns.target_metric: Metric whose baseline forecasts are built.
columns.target_segments: Segments whose baselines are built independently.
columns.seasonal_period_days: Precommitted seasonal period for seasonal naive baseline.
columns.embargo_dates: Dates that exist near cutoff but cannot become training anchors.
columns.primary_baseline_model: Baseline model that future candidate models must beat.
columns.baseline_policy: Comparison and trace policy for future model evaluation.
columns.models: List of naive, seasonal naive, drift, and moving average baseline definitions.
columns.quality_gates: Checks that must pass before candidate forecast models can be compared to the baselines.

decomposition_spec.grain: one decomposition diagnostic setup for a forecast origin
generation_rules: decomposition uses complete training rows only
the STL seasonal period is fixed at seven days from the calendar contract
short tiny history can support diagnostic component checks but not a forecast accuracy claim

columns.forecast_id: Forecast scenario that the decomposition belongs to.
columns.source_table: Regular daily source table used for component extraction.
columns.cutoff_contract_id: Temporal leakage/cutoff contract that defines origin and training window.
columns.baseline_id: Baseline forecast setup that remains the comparison floor after decomposition.
columns.target_metric: Metric whose historical series is decomposed.
columns.method: Decomposition method used by the report.
columns.residual_diagnostics: Residual quality thresholds and reconstruction tolerance.
columns.interpretation_policy: Rules that prevent treating decomposition as forecast proof.
columns.quality_gates: Checks that must pass before component diagnostics can be handed to model lessons.

statsmodels_model_spec.grain: one declared statsmodels candidate forecast setup for a forecast origin
generation_rules: ETS and ARIMA candidates are declared before evaluation
ARIMA order and seasonal_order are fixed explicitly rather than discovered by auto-search
model selection is deferred until rolling-origin backtests and forecast metrics exist

columns.forecast_id: Forecast scenario that the model candidates belong to.
columns.source_table: Regular daily source table used for fitting.
columns.cutoff_contract_id: Temporal leakage/cutoff contract that defines origin, training window, embargo, and horizon.
columns.baseline_id: Baseline forecast setup that remains the comparison floor.
columns.decomposition_id: Decomposition diagnostic used as upstream evidence, not selection proof.
columns.target_metric: Metric whose candidate forecasts are built.
columns.target_segments: Segments whose models are fit independently.
columns.embargo_dates: Dates between training and horizon that model forecasts must skip.
columns.primary_baseline_model: Baseline model used for library-vs-baseline comparison.
columns.uses_exogenous_calendar_features: Whether candidate models consume known future calendar regressors.
columns.selection_policy: Rules that forbid auto-modeling and in-sample model choice.
columns.candidate_models: Explicit ETS and ARIMA/SARIMAX candidate definitions.
columns.residual_diagnostics: Residual warning thresholds reported per fitted model.
columns.quality_gates: Checks that must pass before model forecasts can be handed to backtesting lessons.

backtesting_spec.grain: one rolling-origin backtest setup with multiple historical cutoffs
generation_rules: backtest origins are chronological and use fixed three-day historical horizons in the tiny profile
each split has an explicit one-day embargo gap between training_end and first_forecast_date
models are refit independently for every origin rather than reusing the final forecast fit
model selection is deferred because the tiny profile has fewer than the required number of origins for a decision claim

columns.forecast_id: Forecast scenario whose model candidates are evaluated historically.
columns.source_table: Historical complete series used for backtest training and actuals.
columns.upstream_model_run_id: Statsmodels candidate run whose model definitions are reused.
columns.baseline_id: Baseline setup that provides the primary comparison model.
columns.primary_baseline_model: Baseline model used as the comparison floor.
columns.candidate_model_ids: Statsmodels candidate model ids to refit on each origin.
columns.baseline_model_ids: Baseline model ids emitted in the same backtest table.
columns.final_forecast_horizon_days: Final business forecast horizon from the scenario.
columns.backtest_horizon_days: Historical horizon length used by the tiny profile.
columns.gap_days: Number of embargo days between each training window and horizon.
columns.minimum_origins_for_model_selection: Minimum origin count required before a selection claim is allowed.
columns.retraining_policy: Rules for refitting models at each origin.
columns.split_plan: Chronological expanding and rolling-origin split definitions.
columns.quality_gates: Checks that must pass before forecast metrics can consume backtest outputs.

forecast_metric_spec.grain: one forecast metric evaluation policy for rolling-origin backtest outputs
generation_rules: MAE, RMSE, MAPE, sMAPE, WAPE and MASE are required output metrics
weighted MASE is the primary leaderboard metric because segments have different scales
MAPE and sMAPE are diagnostic-only and must be blocked when zero or tiny denominators appear
tiny profile leaderboard is diagnostic because the upstream backtest still has warning-level limitations

columns.forecast_id: Forecast scenario whose backtests are evaluated.
columns.backtest_id: Rolling-origin backtest package that provides forecast errors.
columns.source_table: Backtest error table consumed by the metric evaluator.
columns.history_table: Historical series used to calculate MASE denominators.
columns.split_manifest: Split manifest used to find training windows for scaled errors.
columns.target_segments: Segments included in segment-level and weighted leaderboard metrics.
columns.primary_baseline_model: Baseline model that candidates must beat.
columns.candidate_model_ids: Candidate models that can appear in the leaderboard.
columns.required_metrics: Forecast metrics that must be emitted.
columns.primary_metric: Leaderboard metric used for model ordering.
columns.seasonal_period_days: Seasonal lag used for MASE denominator calculations.
columns.segment_weights: Weights used by the primary weighted MASE leaderboard.
columns.horizon_weighting: Policy for horizon-step coverage and weighting.
columns.percentage_metric_policy: Rules that keep MAPE and sMAPE diagnostic-only and denominator-safe.
columns.leaderboard_policy: Rules that compare candidates to the primary baseline without overclaiming tiny backtests.
columns.quality_gates: Checks that must pass before metric outputs can be used downstream.

prediction_interval_spec.grain: one prediction interval calibration policy for rolling-origin forecast errors
generation_rules: residual quantile, residual bootstrap and model-based normal intervals are emitted for the same model, segment and horizon grain
coverage is checked empirically on rolling-origin backtest rows rather than trusted from a model formula
final 28-day forecast intervals reuse the last calibrated tiny horizon after day 3 and must carry a warning
tiny profile intervals are diagnostic because backtest origin count and horizon are too small for a production SLA claim

columns.forecast_id: Forecast scenario whose point forecasts receive intervals.
columns.backtest_id: Rolling-origin backtest package that provides historical forecast errors.
columns.metric_evaluation_id: Forecast metric package used as upstream evidence.
columns.model_run_id: Statsmodels candidate forecast run whose final forecasts are intervalized.
columns.baseline_id: Baseline forecast package used for primary baseline intervals.
columns.source_errors_table: Backtest error table used for residual calibration.
columns.final_baseline_forecasts: Final baseline forecast table used to intervalize the primary baseline.
columns.final_candidate_forecasts: Final candidate forecast table used to intervalize ETS and ARIMA candidates.
columns.target_metric: Metric whose forecast intervals are calibrated.
columns.interval_model_ids: Baseline and candidate model ids that must receive intervals.
columns.primary_interval_method: Method used for the required uncertainty handoff.
columns.coverage_target: Nominal empirical coverage target for prediction intervals.
columns.minimum_backtest_rows_per_group: Minimum calibration rows for each model, segment and horizon group.
columns.minimum_origins_for_coverage_claim: Minimum origin count required before coverage can be treated as production evidence.
columns.calibration_grain: Keys used to calibrate residual distributions.
columns.horizon_policy: Rules for applying short backtest horizons to longer final forecast horizons.
columns.methods: Declared residual, bootstrap and model-based interval methods.
columns.interval_policy: Rules distinguishing prediction intervals from confidence intervals and requiring uncertainty statements.
columns.quality_gates: Checks that must pass before interval outputs can be shipped downstream.
